headline: Mental Health First Aid Training Reaches Only 2% of Workplaces
context: Workplace mental health first aid remains rare despite rising rates of employee psychological distress.

cards.big: Only 2% of Malaysian workplaces have employees trained in mental health first aid
cards.sub: Workplace mental health issues cost Malaysian economy RM14.5 billion annually in lost productivity

cards.big: Employee depression and anxiety increased 34% between 2022 and 2025 per SOCSO claims data
cards.sub: Mental health claims now represent the fastest-growing category of occupational health issues

cards.big: Employers are not required to provide any mental health support under current labor laws
cards.sub: Employment Act 1955 addresses physical safety but not psychological wellbeing

cards.big: Australia mandates Mental Health First Aid training in workplaces over 50 employees since 2022
cards.sub: Mandatory training creates baseline capacity for early identification and support

cards.big: Workplaces that train staff in CPR but not mental health first aid address only half the crisis
Should workplaces above 50 employees be required to have trained mental health first aiders
